title: Pseudopanax lessonii
taxonomy: Pseudopanax lessonii is a member of the family Araliaceae. This family is part of the larger order Apiales. Pseudopanax lessonii's classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Pseudopanax lessonii, also known as the Red Pandan or Five Finger, has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are said to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds.
